creator: diizzy@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Stephen Hurd from comment #2)
https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=246174

The cpu feature detection in gmp is a bit broken at times it seems however I think you might be able to get most optimization if you define CPUTYPE?= in /etc/make.conf . In your case a safe bet should/ be CPUTYPE?= ivybridge however the downside is that this doesn't enable avx2 instructions by default but on the other hand I don't think avx2 is enabled by using default settings anyway.

Having a very quick look you might be running into TSX issues as this feature was disabled by Intel in later microcode updates. I'm not sure if GCC/llvm have updated this feature change in their cpu specific switches and I don't see anything in gmp that reflects on that (if that's even the actual cause)

https://gmplib.org/repo/gmp/file/tip/configure.ac#l1690
https://gmplib.org/repo/gmp/file/tip/config.guess#l787

Best regards,
Daniel
